title: Velvety Sour Egyptian Dill Pickle Stir-Fry
description: A velvety and tangy dish that captures the nostalgia of classic Egyptian cuisine, featuring fresh dill pickles in a sugar-free friendly stir-fry.

ingredients.name: 1 baguette slice
ingredients.notes: use whole wheat for added nutrition

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: or any preferred neutral-flavored cooking oil

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: ensure it’s sugar-free if you're keeping the recipe sugar-free-friendly

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.notes: to enhance flavors without overwhelming them

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons honey (optional, for a sweeter taste)
ingredients.notes: adjust as per preference and sugar-free status

instructions: Generously butter one side of the baguette slice and place it cut-side-down on a baking sheet.
In a medium-sized pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the dill pickle slices, garlic, lemon juice, cumin, and black pepper to the pan. Stir occasionally for about 3 minutes until heated through and slightly softened.
If using honey, add it now and stir well until fully combined with the pickle mixture.
Pour the pickle mixture evenly over one side of the baguette slice that’s buttered-side-up in the baking sheet.
Bake in preheated oven for 5-7 minutes or until golden brown on the underside.
Gently flip the baguette to the other side, and continue baking for an additional 3-4 minutes.
Remove from oven and let cool slightly before serving with a side of fresh pickles if desired.
